message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 6 Projects committed and pushed, ready for review">
Step 6 (Projects, US-GTD-4) implementation is committed and pushed.

**Commit:** `0143878` on `main`
**CI:** Waiting for green.

**What was implemented:**
- `Project` domain type as enum state machine (Active/Completed/Dropped) with 13 unit tests
- `ProjectId` newtype wrapper
- Migration: `projects` table with FK from `next_actions.project_id`
- `NextAction` updated with `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` and `new_for_project()` constructor
- Project repository: full CRUD + stalled project detection + project next action count
- Project service: add, list, complete, delete, edit title, get detail, add next action to project
- Routes: `/projects` (list + add), `/projects/{id}` (detail), `/projects/{id}/complete`, `/projects/{id}/delete`, `/projects/{id}/edit`, `/projects/{id}/next-actions` (add action to project), `/projects/{id}/item` (HTMX fragment)
- Templates: projects list, project item, project edit, project detail, project next action item
- CSS: full project component styles following existing patterns
- Focus management JS: `project-focus.js` following HTMX focus protocol
- Dashboard: now shows real projects count and stalled projects count
- Glossary: Project types and error types marked as Implemented
- 28 new integration tests covering all CRUD, HTMX, accessibility labels, semantic markup, auth isolation, dashboard integration

**Pipeline results (local):**
- `cargo fmt`: clean
- `cargo clippy`: clean  
- 183 Rust tests (70 unit + 113 integration): all green
- 11 Playwright e2e tests: all green

**Acceptance criteria addressed (US-GTD-4):**
- [x] Projects list shows all active projects
- [x] Each project shows title and active next action count
- [x] Stalled projects flagged with "No next actions" label (text, not color alone)
- [x] Project detail page with linked next actions (active and completed)
- [x] Add project directly (not through inbox)
- [x] Add next action to project with context selection
- [x] Complete a project
- [x] Drop a project (delete removes from DB)
- [x] Edit project title
- [x] Delete project
- [x] Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances
- [x] All interactive elements keyboard operable
- [x] Semantic `<ul>` with `role="list"` for screen readers
- [x] Heading hierarchy: `<h1>` project title, `<h2>` sections
- [x] Accessible labels including project title on all actions

Please review at commit `0143878`.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- I have a complete picture now. Let me send my review.

input.content:
## Product Review: Step 6 -- Projects (US-GTD-4)

I've reviewed the implementation against all US-GTD-4 acceptance criteria. Thorough work -- this is a big step and it covers a lot of ground.

### Acceptance Criteria Checklist (US-GTD-4)

1. **Projects list shows all active projects** -- MET. `list_active_projects()` fetches active-only. Template renders in a `<ul>`.

2. **Each project shows its title and the count of next actions** -- MET. `project_item.html` shows title as a link and `{{ project.next_action_count }} action(s)` with proper pluralization. Good detail.

3. **User can view a project to see all its linked next actions and completed actions** -- MET. `project_detail.html` has two sections: "Next Actions" (active) and "Completed Actions" (completed, shown only if non-empty). Each action shows title and context. Proper heading hierarchy (`<h1>` project title, `<h2>` section headings).

4. **Projects without a next action are flagged (stalled projects)** -- MET. `is_stalled` flag when `next_action_count == 0`. Shown as "No next actions" text label in the list AND a stalled notice on the detail page ("This project has no next actions. Add one to keep it moving forward."). Uses text, not color alone. Good accessibility.

5. **User can add a project directly** -- MET. Add form on `/projects` with title input.

6. **User can add a next action directly to a project** -- MET. Add form on project detail page with title + context select. Posts to `/projects/{id}/next-actions`.

7. **User can complete a project** -- MET. Complete button per project in list view.

8. **User can delete a project** -- MET. Delete button per project in list view.

9. **User can edit a project title** -- MET. Inline edit via hx-get/hx-post, same pattern as next actions.

10. **Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances** -- MET. All forms have `action` attributes. Non-HTMX paths redirect.

### Dashboard Integration

Learned from the Step 4 lesson -- the dashboard now shows live projects count AND stalled projects count. Both `projects_count` and `stalled_projects_count` are dynamically fetched. This is exactly right.

### Domain Model

The `Project` enum with Active/Completed/Dropped is clean. `drop_project()` is the right name for the Dropped transition. The `from_parts` precedence (completed wins over dropped) is a sensible invariant.

The `new_for_project()` constructor on `NextAction` cleanly separates project-linked vs standalone actions. Good.

### Empty States

Projects list empty state: "No projects yet. Projects are outcomes that need more than one step. You can create one here or during inbox processing." -- Good. Teaches GTD in plain language.

Project detail stalled notice: "This project has no next actions. Add one to keep it moving forward." -- Good. Tells the user what to do next.

### BLOCKING

**B1: Next Actions page does not show associated project name for project-linked actions.**

US-GTD-3 acceptance criterion #4 says: "Each action shows its title, context, **and associated project (if any)**." Now that projects exist (Step 6), the Next Actions page should show the project name next to actions that are linked to a project. Currently `next_action_item.html` shows only title and context -- no project information.

When a user adds a next action to a project via the project detail page, that action appears in both the project view and the Next Actions list. But in the Next Actions list, there is no way to tell which project it belongs to. This matters because during the "Engage" phase (GTD Stage 5), users scan their Next Actions by context and need to know the project context to prioritize.

**Suggested fix**: Add a project name span to `next_action_item.html` (similar to the context badge) that shows when the action has a project. The `NextActionView` struct in `routes/next_actions.rs` would need a `project_name: Option<String>` field, populated by looking up the project title.

### Minor

**M1: The "Stalled Projects" dashboard link and the "Projects" dashboard link both go to `/projects`.**

The dashboard shows "Projects: X items" and "Stalled Projects: Y items" as separate stats, but both link to `/projects`. It would be useful if "Stalled Projects" linked to a filtered view (e.g., `/projects?stalled=true`), but this is a nice-to-have and not needed for Step 6. Just noting it for awareness.

### Summary

One BLOCKING item: B1 (show project name on Next Actions page for project-linked actions). One minor. Once B1 is addressed, you have my consensus.
input.summary: Product review Step 6: 1 blocking (project name on next actions), 1 minor

### Minor

**M1: The "Stalled Projects" dashboard link and the "Projects" dashboard link both go to `/projects`.**

The dashboard shows "Projects: X items" and "Stalled Projects: Y items" as separate stats, but both link to `/projects`. It would be useful if "Stalled Projects" linked to a filtered view (e.g., `/projects?stalled=true`), but this is a nice-to-have and not needed for Step 6. Just noting it for awareness.

### Summary

One BLOCKING item: B1 (show project name on Next Actions page for project-linked actions). One minor. Once B1 is addressed, you have my consensus."
  }
}

content.text:
I've sent my Step 6 product review. Summary:

**9 out of 10 US-GTD-4 acceptance criteria are met.** The implementation is solid -- good domain model, stalled project detection, dashboard integration (lesson learned from Step 4), clean empty states.

**One BLOCKING item**: The Next Actions page does not show the associated project name for project-linked actions. US-GTD-3 criterion #4 explicitly requires "Each action shows its title, context, and associated project (if any)." Now that projects exist, this needs to be wired up -- a project name badge on `next_action_item.html` similar to the context badge.

**One Minor**: Both "Projects" and "Stalled Projects" dashboard links go to `/projects` -- a filtered view would be nice but is not needed now.

Waiting for B1 to be addressed before giving consensus.
